name: mcp-c
vision: Context-efficient CLI protocol for AI agents. Progressive discovery (manifest → group → schema) + output envelope. Runtime reads OpenAPI 3.x and exposes as CLI that speaks the protocol.

tasks.title: Initialize TypeScript project with build tooling
tasks.description: Set up the mcp-c project as a TypeScript npm package. Initialize tsconfig.json with strict mode, ESM output (target ES2022, module NodeNext). Set up src/ directory structure: src/index.ts (CLI entry), src/parser/, src/discovery/, src/executor/, src/output/, src/auth/, src/config/. Add build script with tsc. Add bin field in package.json pointing to dist/index.js. Add shebang (#!/usr/bin/env node) to entry. Rename package from 'tocli' to 'mcp-c' in package.json. Install dependencies: commander, yaml, typescript, @types/node. Install dev dependencies: vitest. Set up vitest config. Keep tiktoken as dependency for the bench.
acceptance: npm run build compiles without errors
npx vitest run passes (even if 0 tests, config must work)
package.json name is 'mcp-c'
package.json has bin field pointing to dist/index.js
tsconfig.json exists with strict: true
src/index.ts exists with shebang and placeholder

hints: Use ESM (type: module in package.json)
Target ES2022 for top-level await support
Keep bench/benchmark.mjs and github_mcp_tools.json as-is
Add scripts: build, test, dev (ts-node or tsx for dev)

tasks.title: Create OpenAPI 3.x spec loader (file + URL)
tasks.description: Create src/parser/loader.ts that loads an OpenAPI 3.x spec from a local file path (YAML or JSON) or a remote URL. Use the 'yaml' package for YAML parsing and native fetch for URLs. Return the parsed object. Validate that it has openapi field starting with '3.' and has paths and info. Throw clear error messages for invalid specs, missing files, or network errors. Export a function: loadSpec(source: string): Promise<OpenAPISpec>.
acceptance: Loads YAML spec from local file path
Loads JSON spec from local file path
Loads spec from remote URL (mock in tests)
Validates OpenAPI 3.x version field
Throws descriptive error for invalid/missing specs
Returns typed OpenAPISpec object
Typecheck passes

hints: Define a minimal OpenAPISpec type (info, paths, tags, components, servers)
Use yaml package for YAML, JSON.parse for JSON
Detect format by file extension or content
For URL: use native fetch (Node 18+)
Create a small petstore.yaml fixture in test/fixtures/ for tests

tasks.title: Build operation extractor from OpenAPI paths
tasks.description: Create src/parser/extractor.ts that takes a parsed OpenAPI spec and extracts all operations into an internal representation (IR). For each path+method combination, extract: operationId (or generate from method+path), tag (first tag or 'default'), summary/description, parameters (path/query/header with types), requestBody schema, responses, security requirements. Group operations by tag. Export function: extractOperations(spec: OpenAPISpec): OperationGroup[]. Define all IR types in src/parser/types.ts.
acceptance: Extracts all path+method combinations
Groups operations by first tag
Generates operationId from method+path when missing
Extracts path params, query params, header params
Extracts request body schema
Extracts security requirements
Handles paths with no tags (groups under 'default')
Typecheck passes

hints: OperationGroup: { tag: string, operations: Operation[] }
Operation: { id: string, method: string, path: string, summary: string, params: Param[], body?: BodySchema, security: SecurityReq[] }
Param: { name: string, in: 'path'|'query'|'header', type: string, required: boolean, description: string, enum?: string[] }
Generate operationId: 'get' + '/users/{id}' → 'getUser' (method + last meaningful path segment)
Use petstore fixture from task 2

tasks.title: Implement Phase 1: Manifest generation
tasks.description: Create src/discovery/manifest.ts. Takes OperationGroup[] and spec info, returns a manifest JSON object: { name, version, description, groups: [{ name, description, commands: count }], _meta: { protocol: 'mcp-c/1', total_commands } }. The manifest should be as compact as possible — only group names, one-line descriptions, and command counts. Export function: generateManifest(groups: OperationGroup[], info: SpecInfo): Manifest.
acceptance: Generates manifest with all groups from spec tags
Each group has name, description, command count
Includes _meta with protocol version
Manifest is compact (verify token count < 600 for petstore)
Typecheck passes

hints: Group description comes from tag description in spec, or 'Manage {tag}' as fallback
Use the IR types from parser/types.ts
Keep output minimal — every extra field costs tokens

tasks.title: Implement Phase 2: Group detail generation
tasks.description: Create src/discovery/group.ts. Takes an OperationGroup and returns the group detail JSON: { group, commands: [{ name, description, method, hint, args? }] }. hint is 'read-only' for GET/HEAD, 'write' for POST/PUT/PATCH, 'destructive' for DELETE. args lists required path params. Export function: generateGroupDetail(group: OperationGroup): GroupDetail.
acceptance: Lists all commands in the group
Each command has name, description, method, hint
hint correctly maps HTTP methods to read-only/write/destructive
args contains required path parameter names
Typecheck passes

hints: Command name derives from operationId, simplified (e.g., 'listPets' → 'list')
Strip the tag prefix from operationId if present

tasks.title: Implement Phase 3: Command schema generation
tasks.description: Create src/discovery/command.ts. Takes an Operation and returns the full command schema JSON: { command: 'group.name', description, params: [{ name, type, required, description, enum?, default? }], auth: { required, scheme }, output?: { type } }. Maps OpenAPI types to compact types (string, integer, number, boolean, enum, string[]). Export function: generateCommandSchema(op: Operation, groupName: string): CommandSchema.
acceptance: Generates full param list from path, query, header params
Includes request body fields as params
Maps OpenAPI types correctly (string, integer, array, enum)
Sets required flag from spec
Includes auth info from security requirements
Uses compact type notation (enum instead of { type: string, enum: [] })
Typecheck passes

hints: Flatten requestBody properties into params list
For body params, mark them with source: 'body' so executor knows
Auth scheme detection: bearer token, apiKey, basic from securitySchemes

tasks.title: Wire --discover flag to CLI entry point
tasks.description: Update src/index.ts to handle the --discover flag. Use commander.js to set up the root command with --spec and --discover options. When --discover is called: load spec, extract operations, then: no args → manifest (phase 1), one arg → group detail (phase 2), two args → command schema (phase 3). Output JSON to stdout. If --spec is not provided and no .mcp-crc config exists, show error with usage hint.
acceptance: mcp-c --spec petstore.yaml --discover outputs manifest JSON
mcp-c --spec petstore.yaml --discover pets outputs group detail JSON
mcp-c --spec petstore.yaml --discover pets list outputs command schema JSON
All outputs are valid JSON to stdout
Error message when --spec is missing and no config
Typecheck passes

hints: Use commander: program.option('--spec <path>', 'OpenAPI spec').option('--discover [args...]', 'Progressive discovery')
Parse --discover args: 0 args=manifest, 1 arg=group, 2 args=command
Use JSON.stringify with no indent for compact output

tasks.title: Create HTTP executor for API calls
tasks.description: Create src/executor/http.ts. Takes an Operation, resolved params (from CLI flags), auth config, and base URL, then makes the actual HTTP request. Builds the URL from path template + path params, adds query params, sets headers, sends body as JSON. Returns { status, headers, data }. Uses native fetch. Export function: executeRequest(op: Operation, params: Record<string,any>, auth: AuthConfig, baseUrl: string): Promise<HttpResponse>.
acceptance: Substitutes path params in URL template (/pets/{petId} → /pets/123)
Appends query params to URL
Sets custom headers from header params
Sends JSON body for POST/PUT/PATCH
Sets auth header (Bearer token, API key)
Returns status code, headers, and parsed JSON body
Handles non-JSON responses gracefully
Typecheck passes

hints: Use native fetch (Node 18+)
Base URL comes from spec servers[0].url or --base-url flag
Content-Type: application/json for body requests
Handle response: try JSON.parse, fallback to text

tasks.title: Build dynamic commander.js commands from OpenAPI operations
tasks.description: Create src/executor/commander-builder.ts. Takes OperationGroup[] and builds commander.js commands dynamically. Each tag becomes a command group, each operation becomes a subcommand with flags matching the operation params. Path params become required arguments, query/header params become options, body fields become options with --data prefix. When a command is invoked, collect all flag values and call executeRequest. Export function: buildCommands(program: Command, groups: OperationGroup[], config: RuntimeConfig): void.
acceptance: Creates command groups matching spec tags
Creates subcommands matching operations
Path params are required arguments or required options
Query params are optional flags
Required params are marked as required in commander
Enum params show choices in help
Boolean params are flags (no value needed)
Typecheck passes

hints: Use commander: program.command('pets').command('list').option('--limit <n>', 'Max results')
For enums: .choices(['available', 'pending', 'sold'])
For required: .requiredOption('--owner <owner>', 'Repository owner')
Connect the action handler to executeRequest via closure

tasks.title: Implement output formatters (json, envelope, table, quiet)
tasks.description: Create src/output/formatters.ts. Implement output formatting based on --output flag. Modes: 'json' (compact JSON to stdout), 'pretty' (default for humans, colorized JSON), 'envelope' (summary + data + _meta), 'table' (tabular for arrays), 'quiet' (only exit code). For envelope mode: generate a summary string from the response (e.g., 'Found N items', 'Created resource #id'), truncate data if > --max-items, include _meta with count/total/truncated. Export function: formatOutput(data: any, options: OutputOptions): string.
acceptance: json mode outputs compact JSON (no indent)
pretty mode outputs indented JSON with syntax highlighting
envelope mode wraps response in { summary, data, _meta }
envelope summary is auto-generated from response shape
envelope respects --max-items truncation
table mode formats arrays as aligned columns
quiet mode outputs nothing (relies on exit code)
Typecheck passes

hints: For summary generation: detect array (Found N items) vs object (Resource #id) vs error
For table: use simple padding, no dependency needed
For colors: use ANSI escape codes, respect NO_COLOR env var
Truncation: slice array to max-items, set truncated: true in _meta

tasks.title: Wire output flags to CLI and executor pipeline
tasks.description: Update src/index.ts and src/executor/commander-builder.ts to add global flags: --output (json|pretty|envelope|table|quiet), --max-items <n>, --verbose, --quiet. Wire the executor response through the formatter before printing. Default to 'pretty' for TTY, 'json' for piped output. Add --verbose to print full request/response (method, URL, headers, status, body).
acceptance: --output json produces compact JSON
--output envelope produces envelope format
--max-items 3 truncates array responses
--verbose shows request details to stderr
--quiet suppresses output
Auto-detects TTY for default format
Typecheck passes

hints: Use process.stdout.isTTY to detect terminal
Verbose output goes to stderr so stdout stays clean for piping
Global options: program.option('--output <format>', 'Output format').option('--max-items <n>', 'Limit items')

tasks.title: Implement auth flag handling and spec-based detection
tasks.description: Create src/auth/flags.ts. Add global auth flags to CLI: --token <token>, --api-key <key>, --auth-header <header>. Detect auth scheme from OpenAPI spec securitySchemes (bearer, apiKey, basic). Build an AuthConfig object that the executor uses. Priority: flags > env vars > config file (config file comes in next task). Support $VARIABLE syntax in config values to read from environment. Export function: resolveAuth(flags: AuthFlags, spec: OpenAPISpec, env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv): AuthConfig.
acceptance: --token sets Bearer auth header
--api-key sets X-API-Key header (or as detected from spec)
Detects auth scheme from spec securitySchemes
Environment variables are resolved ($API_TOKEN → process.env.API_TOKEN)
Flags override env vars
Returns typed AuthConfig used by executor
Typecheck passes

hints: AuthConfig: { type: 'bearer'|'apiKey'|'basic'|'none', value: string, headerName?: string }
OpenAPI securitySchemes: { type: 'http', scheme: 'bearer' } or { type: 'apiKey', in: 'header', name: 'X-API-Key' }
Env var resolution: replace $VAR or ${VAR} patterns with process.env values

tasks.title: Implement persistent auth config (login/logout/status)
tasks.description: Create src/auth/config.ts and src/auth/commands.ts. Persistent config stored in ~/.config/mcp-c/auth.json. Support multiple profiles (default + named). Commands: 'mcp-c auth login' (prompts or uses --token flag, saves to config), 'mcp-c auth logout' (clears config), 'mcp-c auth status' (shows current auth). Update resolveAuth to check config file after flags and env vars. Support --profile flag to select named profiles.
acceptance: auth login --token saves token to ~/.config/mcp-c/auth.json
auth logout clears saved credentials
auth status shows current auth info (masked token)
Multiple profiles with --profile flag
Config file is resolved after flags and env vars
Auth priority: flags > env vars > config file
Typecheck passes

hints: Config structure: { profiles: { default: { type, value }, staging: { type, value } } }
Use XDG_CONFIG_HOME or ~/.config/mcp-c/
Mask token in status output: 'sk-...1234'
Use fs.mkdir with recursive for config dir creation
Register auth commands with commander: program.command('auth').command('login')

tasks.title: Implement .mcp-crc config file and init command
tasks.description: Create src/config/rc.ts and src/config/init.ts. The .mcp-crc file (YAML) stores: spec (path or URL), baseUrl (override), auth.type, auth.envVar, environments (mapping name → { baseUrl, auth }). 'mcp-c init' generates a .mcp-crc by asking for spec path (or detecting OpenAPI files in cwd). When .mcp-crc exists, --spec flag is optional. Support --env flag to select environment. Export functions: loadConfig(): RcConfig | null, initConfig(): void.
acceptance: mcp-c init creates .mcp-crc in current directory
.mcp-crc is loaded automatically when present
--spec flag overrides config spec
Supports environments with --env flag
Config values support env var syntax ($API_URL)
Typecheck passes

hints: Search for .mcp-crc up the directory tree (like .gitignore)
YAML format for config (consistent with OpenAPI specs being YAML)

Example .mcp-crc: spec: ./openapi.yaml
baseUrl: https://api.example.com
auth:
  type: bearer
  envVar: API_TOKEN

tasks.title: Wire full pipeline: spec → parse → discover/execute → output
tasks.description: Update src/index.ts to wire the complete pipeline. The CLI should: 1) Load config (.mcp-crc) if present, 2) Parse --spec or use config, 3) Load and parse OpenAPI spec, 4) If --discover: run discovery protocol, 5) Otherwise: build commander commands dynamically from spec, parse CLI args, execute HTTP request, format output. Handle errors gracefully with clear messages. Add --version flag from package.json. Ensure 'npx mcp-c --spec petstore.yaml pets list' works end-to-end.
acceptance: mcp-c --spec petstore.yaml --discover works (manifest)
mcp-c --spec petstore.yaml pets list works (HTTP call + output)
mcp-c --spec petstore.yaml pets create --name Rex works
Error handling for missing spec, invalid spec, network errors
--version shows package version
--help shows usage for root and subcommands
All existing tests still pass
Typecheck passes

hints: The main flow: loadConfig() → loadSpec(source) → extractOperations(spec) → buildCommands(program, groups, config) → program.parse()
For --discover, intercept before program.parse() since it's a meta-command
Use process.exit(1) for errors, process.exit(0) for success

tasks.title: Create integration tests with real petstore spec
tasks.description: Create test/integration/ directory with tests that use the petstore OpenAPI spec fixture. Test the full flow: loading spec → discovery phases → command building. Mock HTTP calls with a test server (use vitest's built-in server or a simple http.createServer). Verify: discovery outputs correct JSON, command execution builds correct URLs, auth headers are set, output formatting works. Ensure bench/benchmark.mjs still runs.
acceptance: Integration test for discovery phase 1 (manifest)
Integration test for discovery phase 2 (group detail)
Integration test for discovery phase 3 (command schema)
Integration test for command execution with mocked HTTP
Integration test for output envelope format
Integration test for auth flag handling
bench/benchmark.mjs still runs successfully
npm test passes (vitest + benchmark)
Typecheck passes

hints: Use a real petstore spec (https://petstore3.swagger.io/api/v3/openapi.json or local fixture)
Mock HTTP with vitest's vi.fn() on global fetch
Test output by capturing stdout
Update package.json test script to run both vitest and benchmark
